question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP or per-user rate limiting in the ThrottleConfiguration (all three buckets are global singletons) to monopolize the entire gasLimitBucket capacity from a single connection, freezing all other users' transactions and causing block delays exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() floor division to craft gas values that are multiples of GAS_SCALE_FACTOR (e.g., gas = 10,000 * N) to consume exactly N tokens per request, and by choosing N equal to the full bucket capacity, drain the gasLimitBucket in a single request and freeze all subsequent transactions for the entire refill window?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the capacity(rateLimit) == refillGreedy(rateLimit, ...) configuration of rateLimitBucket to perform a full-burst attack at the start of each second, consuming all 500 tokens in a single burst and leaving zero capacity for all other users for the remainder of the second, causing repeated block production delays?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between gasLimitRefundPercent=100 and the restore() function to repeatedly consume and restore gas tokens in a tight loop, causing the gasLimitBucket to be in a perpetually contested state where legitimate transactions cannot acquire tokens, effectively freezing transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gas values between 10,001 and 20,000 to consume exactly 1 scaled token each (due to Math.floorDiv), and by sending 500 such requests per second (the full rateLimitBucket capacity), drain 500 gas tokens per second from the gasLimitBucket, causing a slow but sustained depletion that eventually freezes all higher-gas transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket's capacity of 1 token to create a timing attack where they send a request at the exact moment the bucket refills (every 1 second), permanently monopolizing the opcode endpoint and preventing any other user from ever accessing it, causing a sustained freeze of opcode-dependent transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SynchronizationStrategy.SYNCHRONIZED on gasLimitBucket to perform a lock starvation attack by sending many long-running requests that hold the synchronized lock for extended periods, preventing other threads from acquiring the lock and processing their transactions, causing block production delays exceeding 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the global singleton nature of rateLimitBucket to perform a distributed denial-of-service from multiple clients, where each client sends requests at a rate below the detection threshold but collectively they exhaust the 500 RPS limit, causing all legitimate transactions to be frozen and block production delayed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gasLimitBucket.addTokens() in restore() has no upper bound check (Bucket4j may allow tokens to exceed capacity), causing the bucket to accumulate excess tokens over time and then be drained in a single burst, creating unpredictable transaction processing spikes that delay block production?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential check order in ThrottleManagerImpl.throttle() — rateLimitBucket first, then gasLimitBucket — to send requests that pass the rate check but are designed to fail the gas check, causing the rateLimitBucket to be consumed without any productive transaction processing, wasting the entire rate budget and freezing legitimate transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: rateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the refillGreedy strategy's behavior of distributing refill tokens as quickly as possible (rather than at fixed intervals) to create a window where the bucket appears full but is immediately drained, causing other users to experience intermittent transaction freezes that cumulatively delay block production beyond 500% of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gasLimitBucket is a Spring singleton bean initialized once at startup with a fixed capacity, and by exhausting it early in the application lifecycle before legitimate traffic arrives, cause a prolonged transaction freeze that delays the first block production by 500% or more of average block time?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: gasLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the scaleGas() function's behavior where gas values between 1 and 10,000 return 0 tokens consumed, to send a flood of gas=10,000 requests that bypass the gasLimitBucket entirely while still consuming rateLimitBucket tokens, effectively using the full 500 RPS budget without any gas throttling and overwhelming the transaction processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/config/ThrottleConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: opcodeRateLimitBucket()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcodeRateLimitBucket's refillGreedy(1, Duration.ofSeconds(1)) to create a precise timing attack where they send exactly 1 request per second, permanently occupying the single available token and preventing all other users from accessing the opcode endpoint, causing a complete freeze of opcode-based transaction analysis?
